framework: Schelling focal point detection + physical observables
design: 两层观测: 物理层(状态)+ 焦点层(转折)

physical_observables.description: 每天必须更新的三个物理量。告诉你'现在什么状态'

history.note: Day 10, still high
Day 13, IRGC 'most intense operation'
Day 14, IRGC fighters direct engagement
Day 17, TBD

Day 18. US struck 5000+ targets total. Kharg Island precision strike (Mar 14) destroyed 90+ targets. Iran navy 'crushed' per CENTCOM. 20+ weapons systems deployed. IRGC still active but degraded capacity. US casualties 13 total (7 by enemy fire, 6 in Kuwait drone strike). Amnesty reports unlawful school strike killing 100+ children (Mar 17). AJE live tracker: 1,444 dead Iran (state media), 2,000+ cross-region.

Day 18 late. Attack pattern QUALITATIVE SHIFT: (1) Iran fired Sejjil ballistic missile at Israel (first time in war — new weapon system); (2) Iraqi militia drones+rockets hit US embassy Baghdad + luxury hotel in Green Zone + Iraqi oil field; (3) ISW: 'Iranian-backed Iraqi militias continue to attack US forces and interests in Iraq'; (4) CENTCOM confirmed 20+ weapon systems across air/sea/land. Pattern: IRGC direct attacks declining in frequency but PROXY attacks escalating (Iraq militia) + STRATEGIC weapons (Sejjil BM) being deployed. Degraded but adapting, not collapsing.

Day 19. ★ KEY: ISW Mar 16 report confirms 'Iran has not attacked any vessels in the Strait of Hormuz since March 12' — 5+ days without vessel attacks. US F/A-18 engaging targets at extremely low altitude in Chabahar suggests US local air dominance along coast segments. CENTCOM struck multiple Iranian air defense systems (Mar 16 footage). Larijani (SNSC sec-gen) + Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani killed by Israel. Mojtaba inner circle now dominated by hardline IRGC commanders (Taeb, Vahidi, Jafari, Ghalibaf, Rezaei per NYT). Regime restricting internet further (Starlink crackdown, VPN reduction). IRGC direct vessel attack frequency at war low BUT proxy attacks via Iraq militia continue. Hezbollah planning major rocket fire at Israel per IDF. Pattern: IRGC maritime capability significantly degraded, shifting to proxy + strategic weapons.

Day 19 update. No new kinetic data since last check. ISW vessel attack lull now 6 days (since Mar 12). Pentagon presenting Trump daily options to end war — none taken yet (NBC). Araghchi: 'We did not request a ceasefire, but this war must end in a way that it will not be repeated' (NBC/Fars). CENTCOM 5000+ targets struck total. First 6 days cost $11.3B per Pentagon report to Congress. Pattern unchanged: IRGC maritime degraded, proxy vector (Iraq militia) active, strategic weapons (Sejjil BM) deployed.

Day 19 11:00. P0: Iran CONFIRMED renewed missile+drone attacks on Gulf Arabs+Israel (The Hindu Mar 18). Larijani death CONFIRMED by Iran. IDF vows to hunt Mojtaba. USS Ford pulling into port. CENTCOM bunker busters near Hormuz.

Day 19 pipeline. P0 ESCALATION: Iran announced COORDINATED offensive by IRGC Navy + Iranian Army + Hezbollah against Israel and US bases. This BREAKS the 6-day vessel attack lull pattern. (1) 58 hospitalized in Israel from missile strike; (2) Hezbollah+Iran cluster munitions on Haifa area; (3) Iran again targeted Gulf countries with missiles+drones; (4) USS Ford pulling into port after fire (laundry area, 2 sailors injured) — reduced US carrier capability; (5) CENTCOM bunker busters near Hormuz (Mar 17). Qualitative shift: from degraded/adapting to COORDINATED MULTI-FRONT OFFENSIVE. Central command still functioning despite tactical decentralization. AJE death toll: 1,444 Iran (state media), 15+ Israel, 13 US, 20+ Gulf states.

Day 19 evening pipeline. COORDINATED OFFENSIVE CONFIRMED multi-source: (1) Iran coordinated offensive by IRGC Navy + Army + Hezbollah (Wikipedia); (2) Tel Aviv missile barrage — 2 killed, 58 hospitalized (Dawn/AFP); (3) Cluster munitions on Haifa area (Wikipedia/Lebanon war page); (4) Gulf countries targeted again with missiles/drones, oil prices rising (Wikipedia); (5) Iranian projectile near Australian Al Minhad Air Base UAE (LiveMint); (6) CENTCOM 5000-lb bunker busters on hardened SSM sites along Iran coast near Hormuz (TimesNow/CENTCOM); (7) USS Ford pulling into port = reduced carrier capability; (8) CENTCOM confirmed 20+ weapon systems across air/sea/land; (9) F/A-18 engaging at extremely low altitude near Chabahar. Pattern: 6-day vessel attack lull BROKEN. Iran choosing to spend remaining strategic reserves on coordinated multi-front offensive rather than slow attrition. This is a use-it-or-lose-it calculus.

Day 19 late. 🔴 ENERGY RUBICON CROSSED: (1) Israel/US coordinated strike on SOUTH PARS gas field (world's largest, shared w/ Qatar) + Asaluyeh oil facilities (Bloomberg/NYT/JPost/AJE/Guardian/Reuters); (2) Israel killed Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib — 3rd top official in 48hrs (AP/NYT/CNN); (3) Iran IRGC issued SPECIFIC evacuation orders for Gulf energy targets: Saudi Samref+Jubail, UAE Al Hosn, Qatar Mesaieed+Ras Laffan (Reuters); (4) Aramco evacuating Samref personnel (OilPrice); (5) Mojtaba REJECTED de-escalation — demands US/Israel 'brought to their knees' (Reuters/SMH/CNA); (6) Mossad ground op inside Iran (Al Arabiya single source); (7) Iran using Russian-modified Shaheds (ISW). Pattern: WAR PHASE CHANGE — from military-to-military combat to ENERGY INFRASTRUCTURE WAR. The 3-week tacit truce on energy targets is BROKEN. Iran listing specific Saudi/UAE/Qatar facilities = war may expand from US-Iran to REGIONAL ENERGY CONFLICT.

Day 20 (entering). ENERGY WAR PHASE CONFIRMED MULTI-SOURCE: (1) Israel/US struck South Pars gas field (world largest, shared w/ Qatar) — Bloomberg/NYT/AP/CNN/AJE/Guardian all confirm; (2) Asaluyeh oil facilities also hit; (3) Intelligence Minister Khatib killed by Israel — 4th top official eliminated; (4) IRGC coordinated offensive by Navy + Army + Hezbollah continues; (5) CENTCOM: 7,000+ targets struck (up from 5,000+); (6) Gulf states (Kuwait/Qatar/Saudi/UAE) intercepting missiles and drones daily; (7) Iran using Russian-modified Shahed drones (ISW); (8) Euronews: barrages targeting Gulf region daily; (9) Amnesty: CENTCOM using AI tools for targeting; (10) Turkish Airlines cancelled all regional flights until Mar 19-20. QUALITATIVE ASSESSMENT: War has entered ENERGY INFRASTRUCTURE PHASE. The 3-week tacit truce on energy targets is broken. Both sides now hitting each other economic lifelines. This is the most significant escalation since the war began.

Day 20. 🔴 P0 — ENERGY WAR RETALIATION EXECUTED: (1) Iran STRUCK Ras Laffan Industrial City (Qatar) with missiles — 'extensive damage' confirmed by QatarEnergy (CNBC/AJE/Reuters). World's LARGEST LNG export hub. No casualties reported but infrastructure damage confirmed; (2) Saudi Arabia intercepted 4 ballistic missiles targeting RIYADH (Guardian); (3) UAE intercepted 13 ballistic missiles + 27 drones from Iran, shut gas facilities (AJE); (4) IRGC statement: enemies should 'await the powerful action' of armed forces after South Pars attack (CNN/Newsweek); (5) CENTCOM: 7,000+ targets struck total (AJE); (6) Pentagon reportedly requesting $200B in war funds (Guardian); (7) Dow -750pts, S&P -1.3% on Mar 18 after Fed decision + war escalation (Yahoo Finance); (8) ISW Mar 18: IRGC Navy ammo depot Bushehr + Bandar-e Jask port struck; (9) Araghchi firmly rejects ceasefire (Time). QUALITATIVE ASSESSMENT: War has crossed from ENERGY INFRASTRUCTURE threats to KINETIC ENERGY WAR across Gulf. Iran's IRGC evacuation warnings (Mar 17) for Saudi/UAE/Qatar targets were NOT rhetoric — they were operational targeting notices. The 3-week tacit truce on energy infrastructure is irrevocably broken on BOTH sides. This is the most dangerous escalation since the war began — a full regional energy war is now underway.

Day 20 late. 🔴 P0 — MUTUAL ENERGY DESTRUCTION ACCELERATING: (1) Bloomberg/Reuters: Iran strike damaged 17% of Qatar LNG capacity — recovery 3-5 YEARS (Bloomberg Mar 19); (2) Iran targeted Saudi refinery, UAE gas forced shut, Kuwait fires at 2 energy sites (Reuters Mar 19 comprehensive); (3) Trump on Truth Social: threatened to 'massively blow up the ENTIRE South Pars Gas Field at an amount and strength and power that Iran has never seen' (NPR Mar 19) — this is NOT rhetoric, it's a MILITARY THREAT given prior South Pars strike was executed; (4) ★ US-ISRAEL COORDINATION DISPUTE: Israeli officials say South Pars attack was 'coordinated with US' — Trump claims he 'knew nothing' (Guardian/Reuters Mar 19); (5) ★ BESSENT DESPERATE MEASURE: floated UNSANCTIONING 130-140M bbl of Iranian oil already on water to lower prices (Bloomberg/NYT/Fox Business Mar 19) — bombing a country AND unsanctioning its oil simultaneously = logical incoherence peak; (6) Pentagon formally requesting $200B supplemental war funds (AP/WashPost/CNBC confirmed Mar 19); Hegseth: figure 'could move', 'takes money to kill bad guys'; (7) Senate voted DOWN War Powers resolution 53-47 on Mar 18 — only Rand Paul (R) crossed, Fetterman (D) voted against; 4 more Dem resolutions eligible in 10 days (NYT/Politico/Guardian); (8) Brent SPIKED to $119/bbl intraday (BBC/Reuters Mar 19) before pulling back to ~$112; UK gas +19% (BBC); (9) India CONDEMNED Gulf energy strikes as 'unacceptable' (Guardian Mar 19); (10) S&P 500 -0.85% more on Mar 19 after -1.36% on Mar 18; Dow new 2026 low, worst month since 2022 (CNBC/Yahoo); (11) AJE live tracker: casualties rising across region; (12) CENTCOM 7,000+ targets struck total. QUALITATIVE ASSESSMENT: War entering MUTUAL ASSURED ENERGY DESTRUCTION phase. Both sides are now systematically destroying each other's energy infrastructure — Iran hitting Gulf allies' LNG/refining, US/Israel hitting Iran's gas/oil. The 17% Qatar LNG damage for 3-5 years means PERMANENT global energy supply reduction regardless of ceasefire timing. Bessent's unsanctioning gambit reveals the administration KNOWS oil prices are politically unsustainable but has NO mechanism to stop the destruction causing them.

Day 20 evening. 🔴 P0 — ENERGY WAR + DIPLOMATIC BREAK: (1) Iran fired 8+ barrages at Israel since midnight Mar 19 (ToI liveblog); (2) Cluster munitions on Ramat Gan — 2 killed in 70s found outside safe room (Wikipedia/AP); (3) Iran retaliatory offensive continues: IRGC Navy + Army + Hezbollah coordinated; (4) ★ DIPLOMATIC RUPTURE: Qatar EXPELLED Iranian military/security attachés — persona non grata, 24hr to leave (Gulf Times/Al Arabiya/ET). First Gulf state to formally break military ties with Iran during this war; (5) Qatar framed as response to 'repeated and blatant Iranian' attacks on energy facilities; (6) QatarEnergy: Ras Laffan 'extensive damage' confirmed — world's largest LNG hub (CNBC/Reuters/AJE); (7) Qatar had halted LNG production since Mar 2 due to earlier drone strikes (CNBC); (8) Brent SURGED 3.71% to $111.36 (LiveMint) — breaking $110 barrier; (9) India Sensex crashed 2,500 pts, ₹12 lakh crore eroded (LiveMint); (10) European equities -2% (year low), Asian stocks -2.8% (Bloomberg); (11) S&P 500 futures flat after previous -1.36% close at 6,624.70 below 200-DMA; (12) CENTCOM 7,000+ targets; US 13 fatalities confirmed (AJE). QUALITATIVE ASSESSMENT: Qatar's diplomatic break is the most significant POLITICAL development in weeks — it destroys Iran's last Gulf mediator channel and forces China's hand (Qatar = ~20% of China's LNG). The energy war is now SELF-REINFORCING: infrastructure damage → price spikes → political pressure → more strikes. No off-ramp visible.

history.est: 3+ ENERGY WAR + TRUMP PIVOT ON ENERGY STRIKES
history.note: Day 21 early. 🔴 P0 — DUAL PIVOT: (1) ★ ACTION: Trump told Netanyahu "don't do that" — halt attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure (News18/CNN Mar 19). "We're not doing that anymore." First direct constraint on Israel targeting since war began; (2) ★ ACTION: Iran INTENSIFIED Gulf energy offensive Mar 19 (Reuters/CBC/AJE): hit Saudi Aramco SAMREF refinery in Yanbu (Red Sea), set Qatari LNG facilities ablaze AGAIN, two Kuwaiti oil refineries set on fire. IRGC executing energy war retaliation despite Trump de-escalation; (3) ★ CLAIM: Netanyahu says Iran "no longer can enrich uranium or make ballistic missiles" (AP/Reuters/Yahoo Mar 19) — maximalist victory claim, possible narrative for ceasefire; (4) Iran parliament considering Hormuz transit fees/taxes bill (Reuters Mar 19); (5) 2nd Senate War Powers defeated — 4 more resolutions eligible in 10 days (NYT/Politico); (6) AJE: analysts now seriously considering $150-$200/bbl; (7) Joe Kent (DNI deputy) resigned: "Iran posed no imminent threat" — contradicts casus belli (Guardian). ASSESSMENT: TWO CONTRADICTORY PIVOTS — Trump constraining Israel on energy = first real de-escalation ACTION, while Iran ESCALATING energy attacks on Gulf. Netanyahu uranium claim = possible "mission accomplished" narrative. If energy targets stop BOTH sides, energy war phase could plateau. But Iran asymmetry: nothing left to lose on energy, Gulf states have everything to lose.

Day 21. 🔴 P0 — FIRST US STEALTH AIRCRAFT DAMAGED + ENERGY OFFENSIVE CONTINUES: (1) ★★★ F-35A struck by Iranian fire during combat mission over Iran — emergency landing at US base, pilot stable (CNN/Air&Space Forces/Aviationist Mar 19). FIRST confirmed hit on US stealth aircraft in this war — demonstrates IRGC retains some air defense capability despite 'rendered useless' claims; (2) Iran INTENSIFIED Gulf energy strikes Mar 19: Qatari LNG ablaze AGAIN, Saudi Aramco SAMREF refinery (Yanbu/Red Sea) hit by drone, 2 Kuwait refineries on fire (AP/Reuters/AJE/Guardian Mar 19 comprehensive); (3) Vessel set ablaze off UAE coast, another damaged off Qatar (AP); (4) ★ ACTION: Netanyahu confirms halting South Pars attacks at Trump's request — 'Israel will hold off on further attacks on South Pars gas field' (AP Mar 19); (5) Israel struck Yazd airport, Bandar Abbas naval vessel, fuel depots, Shiraz (Alma Center daily report Mar 19); (6) CENTCOM 7,000+ targets struck total; 20+ weapon systems; (7) AJE live tracker: 1,444 dead Iran (state media), 18+ Israel, 13 US, 21+ Gulf states; (8) Japan PM Takaichi in DC seeking Trump help on Hormuz (AP/Britannica). QUALITATIVE ASSESSMENT: F-35 hit is STRATEGICALLY significant — shatters 'Iran military rendered useless' narrative (Netanyahu Mar 19). Iran energy retaliation hitting Saudi Red Sea alternative route (Yanbu) = closing the BYPASS as well as the strait. War in MUTUAL ENERGY ANNIHILATION phase with first cracks in US air superiority assumption.

history.est: 3+ TRIDIRECTIONAL ENERGY WAR — IRAN HITTING ISRAEL ENERGY
history.note: Day 21 afternoon. 🔴 P0 — ENERGY WAR NOW TRIDIRECTIONAL: (1) ★★★ Iran struck HAIFA OIL REFINERY (Bazan/ORL) with missile — produces 50% of Israel's domestic fuel supply. Energy Ministry says 'no significant damage' but power briefly disrupted (Reuters/AJE/JPost Mar 19). Energy war now: US/Israel→Iran, Iran→Gulf, AND Iran→Israel; (2) ★★ Iran fired 5 MISSILE SALVOS at Jerusalem + northern Israel within 1 HOUR (ToI Mar 19). Cluster munitions on Haifa. Burning fragment footage. Sustained BM capability demonstrated; (3) ★★ CIA Director Ratcliffe told Senate: war will last 4-6 WEEKS (NYT Mar 19). BBC: 'Pentagon officials said US action could last 4-6 weeks'. FIRST OFFICIAL US DURATION ESTIMATE = 1-3 more weeks from now; (4) Trump says NO ground troops + 'ahead of schedule' (Newsweek/Guardian) — DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS Reuters reinforcement story from same day; (5) IDF hit 130+ targets in western/central Iran in past day (ToI); (6) UAE dismantled Iran/Hezbollah terrorist network (Reuters Mar 20 03:36 UTC); (7) NZ fuel stations 'Out of Stock' (ToI image) — physical fuel shortages reaching OCEANIA; (8) Gabbard testimony: nuclear enrichment 'obliterated', facilities 'buried with cement'; (9) Reuters: fuel cargo prices SMASH RECORD HIGHS — 'largest-ever disruption to global energy supplies'; (10) Netanyahu: Iran 'being decimated', 'unclear who is in charge' (The Hindu). QUALITATIVE ASSESSMENT: Ratcliffe's 4-6 week timeline is the most important FRAMING signal — it means US plans to continue for 1-3 more weeks regardless of exit rhetoric. Haifa refinery hit makes energy war TRIDIRECTIONAL — Iran no longer only hitting Gulf states, now hitting ISRAEL's domestic fuel supply. Trump 'no ground troops' contradicts Reuters reinforcement story = classic ambiguity preservation. Physical fuel shortages (NZ) = energy crisis going from price to AVAILABILITY.

Day 21 evening. 🔴🔴 P0 — MULTILATERAL EXIT FRAMEWORK MATERIALIZING: (1) ★★★★ UNSC Resolution 2817 PASSED — explicitly affirms Iran's attacks breach international law. RARE explicit legal determination (ME Council analysis). G7-level joint statement (UK/France/Germany/Italy/Netherlands/Japan/Canada Mar 19) demands Iran cease mines/drones/missiles, comply with UNSC 2817; (2) ★★★ Arab/Islamic FMs met in RIYADH: demanded Iran cease attacks, comply with UNSC 2817, end proxy support, stop blocking Hormuz (Pakistan Today/CNN); (3) ★★★ CIA Ratcliffe: war will last 4-6 WEEKS total (NYT/BBC) = 1-3 more weeks from Day 21. Pentagon officials confirmed same timeline. FIRST OFFICIAL US DURATION ESTIMATE; (4) ★★ Iran struck Haifa oil refinery — produces 50% Israel domestic fuel. Energy war now TRIDIRECTIONAL: US/Israel→Iran, Iran→Gulf, Iran→Israel (Reuters/AJE Mar 19); (5) ★★ Iran fired 5 missile salvos at Jerusalem + northern Israel within 1 HOUR (ToI). Cluster munitions on Haifa. SUSTAINED BM CAPABILITY; (6) Saudi intercepted missile over Al Jouf on Mar 20 (Wikipedia) — Gulf attacks CONTINUE into Day 21; (7) Overnight strikes hit Bahrain/Kuwait/UAE (Independent); (8) Trump says NO ground troops, 'ahead of schedule' — CONTRADICTS Reuters reinforcement story (Sky News); (9) QatarEnergy force majeure on contracts to Belgium/Italy/South Korea/China — 20% global LNG, 17% capacity damaged 3-5 years; (10) NZ fuel stations 'Out of Stock' — physical shortages reaching Oceania; (11) Brent ~$110, S&P heading for longest weekly losing streak since Mar 2025 (Bloomberg). CRITICAL ASSESSMENT: UNSC 2817 + G7 statement + Arab/Islamic FMs = MULTILATERAL EXIT FRAMEWORK emerging. This provides the 'face-saving scaffolding' I was looking for in fp3. Iran now isolated legally (UNSC) + diplomatically (Arab/Islamic states) + economically (force majeure). Ratcliffe 4-6 week timeline = operational ceiling. The exit is no longer unilateral Trump declaration — it's MULTILATERAL LEGAL FRAMEWORK. EXIT PROBABILITY REVISED: 70% within 14 days (up from 50%).

Day 21 evening. DUAL OPERATIONAL SHIFTS: (1) ★★★ ACTION: A-10 Warthogs now HUNTING Iranian fast attack craft in Strait of Hormuz (Gen. Caine JCOS briefing Mar 19, confirmed DailyMail/TWZ/Aviationist/AirForceTimes). 'Targeting fast-attack watercraft' = first dedicated maritime interdiction mission for strait reopening; (2) ★★ Reuters: US weighing deploying troops to Iran's SHORELINE for Hormuz security — 'thousands of troops' contemplated (AJE/Reuters/RFE-RL Mar 19); (3) ★★ DailyMail: 'US opens new front in battle to reopen Strait of Hormuz' — jets obliterate Iranian ships, take down drones; (4) Iran still conducting tridirectional energy war: Haifa refinery (50% Israel fuel), Gulf states, AND continued multi-front offensive; (5) CENTCOM 7,000+ targets; (6) F-35 damage confirmed (CNN); (7) 13 US fatalities + 6 crew in KC-135 crash (AJE). PATTERN: US strategy BIFURCATING — continuing to degrade Iran (7,000+ targets) while SIMULTANEOUSLY shifting assets toward Hormuz reopening (A-10s + MEU + possible troop deployment). Iran's response: double down on energy infrastructure attacks across ALL vectors. Neither side showing signs of operational exhaustion.
only 2 non-Iran/Russia vessels attempted transit

Day 18. 1000+ cargo ships blocked. 150+ tankers anchored outside. Shadow fleet makes half of transits. Iran says open to all except US/allies. 16 tankers attacked since war started. Trump calls for naval coalition - Japan/Australia refuse, France considering 'purely defensive' escort. US Navy tells shipping: escorts 'not possible for now' (directly contradicts Trump). No allied warships committed.

Day 18 late. No change. Energy Sec Chris Wright confirmed: 'We're simply not ready' for escort operations. CNN analysis calls strait a 'death valley'. Tanker traffic still ~70% below pre-crisis. Key constraint: 5000+ Iranian mines in strait + degraded US minesweep capability. Escorts estimated end-of-March at earliest. Iraq oil field also attacked by militia drones — supply disruption expanding beyond strait.

Day 19. No material change. Iran has not attacked vessels since Mar 12 (ISW confirmed), BUT mines remain + insurance paralyzed + no escort capability. Bessent says US allowing Iranian tankers through. Shadow fleet still half of transits (Bloomberg). 1000+ cargo ships still blocked. US Navy refuses daily escort requests from shipping industry (Reuters). Key: the 5-day vessel attack lull does NOT equal reopening — mines + insurance + escort gap = structural blockade persists regardless of attack frequency. CNBC: allies (Japan/Australia) refuse to send warships; France considering 'purely defensive' option only.

Day 19 update. NYT: 'Europe Has Helped the US Protect Shipping. The Strait of Hormuz Is Different' — Aspides-template discussed but Hormuz mine threat = qualitatively different from Houthi/Red Sea anti-ship missiles. Bessent confirms Iranian tankers transiting (CNBC Mar 16). Guardian: energy prices soared, mines + strikes prevent ships transiting. AJE: Iran allowing safe passage to select countries (China negotiating). Only a handful of non-Iran vessels attempted transit. Structural blockade unchanged.
Day 19. Structural blockade persists. CENTCOM bunker busters near Hormuz = possible prep.

Day 19 pipeline. Windward Mar 16: transit at near-zero levels. USS Ford heading to port after fire = reduced naval capability. Iran renewed Gulf attacks will FURTHER freeze insurance/shipping. Structural blockade: mines+insurance+no escort+renewed attacks = no recovery vector. 1000+ cargo ships still blocked. No allied warships committed (Japan/Australia/France refused).

Day 19 evening. KEY NEW DATA: (1) CNBC Mar 18: only 21 tankers total since war began vs 100+/day pre-crisis; (2) 11 China-linked vessels Mar 1-15 per Lloyd's List (mostly cargo, not mainstream tankers); (3) AJE Mar 18: MarineTraffic recorded 9 transits Sun/Mon vs 5 previous 2 days — SLIGHT UPTICK in selective passage; (4) China shrapnel incident on Mar 12 deterred further Chinese transits (Lloyd's List); (5) Greek Dynacom tankers (Shenlong, Smyrni) tested route with Saudi crude to Mumbai; (6) 400 vessels backlog in Gulf of Oman (Windward); (7) BUT: Iran coordinated offensive + Gulf attacks TODAY will reverse any thaw in selective passage. Iran attacking Gulf states = insurers will further freeze. Structural blockade: mines+insurance+no escort+renewed Gulf attacks = no recovery vector. The slight uptick in trickle traffic is NOISE — the blockade remains functionally total for energy cargo.

Day 19 late. ENERGY WAR ESCALATION destroys any recovery path: (1) South Pars struck = Iran's own gas infrastructure being destroyed; (2) Iran threatening Saudi Samref/Jubail, UAE Al Hosn, Qatar Mesaieed/Ras Laffan = if executed, energy disruption far BEYOND Hormuz blockade; (3) Aramco evacuating = market will re-price shipping risk massively upward; (4) CENTCOM GBU-72 bunker busters on Hormuz coastal sites = physical prep for reopening BUT South Pars/Gulf threats make reopening timeline IRRELEVANT if regional energy infrastructure attacked; (5) Insurance market will freeze further. Assessment: strait recovery decoupled from combat cessation — even if bombing stops, mines+insurance+Gulf infrastructure risk = months to recover.

Day 20. Structural blockade persists AND worsening: (1) Energy war escalation (South Pars + Gulf energy targets threatened) makes recovery even more distant; (2) CENTCOM GBU-72 bunker busters on Hormuz coastal sites (physical prep) BUT irrelevant while energy infrastructure being destroyed regionally; (3) Escort operations "end of March" at earliest per Energy Sec — but mine threat qualitatively different from Red Sea (NYT, TWZ); (4) Wikipedia Hormuz crisis: escort readiness still weeks away; (5) Insurance market will freeze FURTHER after South Pars strike + Gulf energy threats. Assessment: strait recovery now DECOUPLED from combat cessation — even post-ceasefire, mines+insurance+infrastructure damage = months to recover.

history.est: <2 mbd (CATASTROPHIC — regional energy infrastructure under fire)
history.note: Day 20. Strait transit now IRRELEVANT as bottleneck — regional energy infrastructure being destroyed on BOTH sides: (1) Ras Laffan (Qatar) struck by Iran missiles, 'extensive damage' — world's largest LNG hub (CNBC/Reuters/AJE); (2) UAE shut gas facilities after intercepting 13 BMs + 27 drones (AJE); (3) Saudi intercepted 4 BMs targeting Riyadh (Guardian); (4) Windward: transit at near-zero, 400+ vessels backlogged in Gulf of Oman; (5) CNBC Mar 18: only 21 tankers total since war started vs 100+/day pre-crisis; (6) AJE: slight uptick (9 transits Sun/Mon) wiped out by Iran's Gulf energy offensive; (7) Insurance market: functionally dead for Gulf region. Assessment: PARADIGM SHIFT — the question is no longer 'when does the strait reopen' but 'how much Gulf energy infrastructure survives'. Even if fighting stops today, Ras Laffan damage + South Pars damage + mines = MONTHS to restore energy flows.

history.est: <2 mbd (METRIC NOW OBSOLETE — production destruction dominates)
history.note: Day 20 late. Transit metric PERMANENTLY SUPERSEDED: (1) Bloomberg: Iran strike damaged 17% of Qatar LNG capacity for 3-5 YEARS — this is production destruction, not transit disruption; (2) Reuters Mar 19: Iranian aerial attacks caused extensive damage across Gulf — Qatar Ras Laffan, Saudi refinery, UAE gas shut, Kuwait fires; (3) India condemned strikes as 'unacceptable', warning of 'worsening global energy flows' (Guardian); (4) Brent $119 intraday (BBC/Reuters) then back to ~$112 — market pricing in PERMANENT supply loss not temporary blockade; (5) UK gas prices +19% in single day (BBC Mar 19); (6) China still in talks with Iran on selective passage (Guardian/AJE) but irrelevant when production facilities are being destroyed; (7) 400+ vessels still backlogged in Gulf of Oman. Assessment: the correct metric is now GULF PRODUCTION CAPACITY SURVIVAL RATE. Qatar lost 17% LNG for 3-5 years. South Pars damaged. UAE gas offline. Even FULL strait reopening cannot restore destroyed production. The war has shifted from BLOCKING energy flows to DESTROYING energy capacity.

Day 20 evening. Transit question SUPERSEDED by production destruction: (1) Qatar expelled Iranian military attachés after Ras Laffan 'extensive damage' — LNG production halted since Mar 2 AND now physical infrastructure damaged (CNBC/Gulf Times); (2) Qatar supplies ~20% of world LNG (ToI); (3) UAE shut gas facilities (AJE); (4) Brent $111.36 (+3.71%) reflects BOTH transit blockade + production loss; (5) WTI-Brent spread widest in 11 years (Reuters) = international crude premium for Gulf risk; (6) Insurance market: functionally dead for entire Gulf region, not just strait; (7) Even allies (Japan/Australia/Europe) refused escort operations (Reuters/CNN). Assessment: the transit metric is now a LAGGING indicator. LEADING indicator is Gulf production capacity survival rate. Ras Laffan damage + South Pars damage = net global energy supply permanently reduced until rebuilt (12-18 months for LNG facilities).

Day 21. Transit metric FULLY OBSOLETE: (1) Iran intensified Gulf energy strikes Mar 19: Saudi Aramco SAMREF refinery (Yanbu/Red Sea) hit, Qatari LNG ABLAZE again, 2 Kuwait refineries ON FIRE (Reuters/CBC/AJE comprehensive); (2) Bloomberg: 17% Qatar LNG capacity damaged 3-5 years — $20B annual revenue lost (QatarEnergy CEO); (3) Iran parliament considering Hormuz transit fees/tolls bill (Reuters Mar 19) — signaling post-war "new strait regime"; (4) Brent $119 intraday before pullback to ~$112 (BNN Bloomberg); (5) Trump admin says escort operations "could soon begin" (CBS/ABC) but irrelevant when production infrastructure being destroyed; (6) No allies committed warships (BBC confirms UK/France/China all refused); (7) US 31st MEU deployed for Hormuz security but mine threat qualitatively different. ASSESSMENT: Production destruction > transit blockade. Even full strait reopening cannot restore destroyed LNG/refining capacity.

history.est: <2 mbd (IRRELEVANT — bypass route now also under fire)
history.note: Day 21. Transit metric FULLY OBSOLETE + BYPASS ATTACKED: (1) AJE Mar 18: 8 non-Iranian vessels detected by Windward, 'nearly double' recent days — minor trickle; (2) CNBC: only 21 tankers since war began vs 100+/day pre-crisis; (3) BUT: Iran drone hit Saudi SAMREF refinery at YANBU (Red Sea) — Saudi Arabia's only crude export outlet bypassing Hormuz (AP/Guardian Mar 19). Iran now attacking the ALTERNATIVE route; (4) Vessel ablaze off UAE coast, another damaged off Qatar (AP); (5) Qatar LNG ablaze AGAIN — production halted since Mar 2, infrastructure being destroyed (AP); (6) US admin says escort ops 'could soon begin' (CBS/TNND) — but mine threat qualitatively different; 31st MEU deployed; (7) Japan PM Takaichi visiting Trump seeking help on Hormuz (AP/Britannica); (8) No allied warships committed (UK/France/China refused, Japan/Australia declined). ASSESSMENT: Iran's Yanbu strike is STRATEGICALLY DEVASTATING — it eliminates Saudi Arabia's Hormuz bypass. Saudi was pumping crude west to Red Sea to avoid strait; Iran hit the endpoint. Now BOTH routes (Hormuz + Red Sea alternative) under fire. Transit metric irrelevant — the correct metric is REGIONAL EXPORT CAPACITY under fire from all vectors.

history.est: <2 mbd (METRIC OBSOLETE — regional production+export under systematic attack)
history.note: Day 21 pipeline. (1) Brent settling $105-107 in Asian Friday session (down from Thu $108.65 settle, after $119 intraday spike) — Netanyahu South Pars halt signal causing partial relief; (2) Gulf News: Middle East crude surges to $131 while Brent at $107, WTI $94 — DUAL MARKET pricing emerging (regional vs international benchmark divergence); (3) US crude heading to Asia via Panama Canal as Iran crisis redraws trade flows (Reuters Mar 20); (4) Israel now claims 'helping reopen Hormuz' (CNBC Mar 19 — Netanyahu statement) — unverified, no physical change; (5) CNBC: 21 tankers total since war began; AJE: 8 non-Iranian vessels (Windward); (6) IEA Mar 2026 report: flows fallen from ~20 mbd to 'minimal volumes' (Global Trade Magazine); (7) Iran parliament considering Hormuz transit fees bill (Reuters). ASSESSMENT: Transit metric fully obsolete. The correct measure is now GULF PRODUCTION CAPACITY SURVIVAL RATE. Qatar 17% LNG lost 3-5yr. South Pars damaged. Saudi bypass (Yanbu) attacked. Kuwait refineries on fire. UAE gas offline. DUAL MARKET PRICING ($131 ME crude vs $107 Brent) = most distorted oil market since 1973. Even full strait reopening cannot restore destroyed production.

history.est: <2 mbd (BUT: A-10 HORMUZ DEPLOYMENT = FIRST REOPENING OPERATIONS)
history.note: Day 21 evening. OPERATIONAL SHIFT TOWARD REOPENING but transit still near-zero: (1) ★★★ A-10 Warthogs hunting Iranian fast attack craft in Hormuz (Gen. Caine Mar 19) — first dedicated maritime interdiction for reopening; (2) ★★ US weighing 'thousands of troops' for Hormuz security, including Iran shoreline deployment (Reuters/AJE Mar 19); (3) DailyMail: US 'obliterating' Iranian ships, downing drones at Hormuz; (4) 31st MEU deployed; (5) CNBC: still only 21 tankers total since war began; (6) MarineTraffic: 9 transits on best recent days (AJE Mar 18); (7) Middle East Forum: 300+ ships stranded INSIDE Gulf; (8) Wikipedia: Maersk/CMA CGM/Hapag-Lloyd all suspended transits; (9) BUT mines remain the CRITICAL obstacle — A-10s address surface threats, NOT mines; UK minesweep drones discussed but no deployment confirmed; (10) Brent pullback ($105-107 Friday Asian) partly reflects A-10/reopening optimism. ASSESSMENT: US shifting from 'destroy Iran' to 'reopen Hormuz' as a PARALLEL mission. A-10s + MEU + possible troops = physical prep. But mine threat is QUALITATIVELY different from surface boats — A-10s can kill boats, cannot clear mines. Recovery timeline: weeks at minimum even in best case. Transit metric still secondary to PRODUCTION DESTRUCTION (Qatar LNG 17% lost 3-5yr, Saudi Yanbu hit, Kuwait refineries).

q3_spr_release_rate.label: SPR实际释放速率(mbd)
q3_spr_release_rate.delivery_timeline: 120 days per DOE, first barrels end of W3

history.note: DOE offers 86M bbl sour crude for sale. Asia 108.6M bbl available immediately
IEA says Asia-Pacific stocks available immediately, Americas/Europe from end of March

US SPR 172M bbl announced. DOE arranged 200M bbl replacement within year (20% more than drawdown). Max pump rate 4.4 mbd. SPR release officially began Mar 16 (FinancialContent). First physical barrels expected ~Mar 21. No delivery confirmation yet. Asia-Pacific reserves available immediately per IEA.

No new data on physical delivery. SPR release D+1 (began Mar 16). CNBC analysis: 400M bbl global release = historic but crude prices still climbing (Brent $103+) because physical delivery takes weeks and market sees strait blockade as potentially longer-lasting. Key metric to watch: DOE Wednesday weekly report (Mar 19) for first drawdown numbers.

SPR release D+2. Still no physical delivery confirmation. First barrels expected ~Mar 21. DOE Wed report (Mar 19) will show first drawdown data. NPR confirms gas up 80¢/month from pre-war, diesel near $5/gal (+$1.34/month). Brent $103/bbl despite 400M bbl release = market pricing in prolonged blockade. Market verdict: SPR release = necessary but insufficient if strait stays closed. Physical rate matters more than announced volume.

SPR D+2.5. No new delivery data. Brent dipped below $100 briefly (Mar 17 Oninvest) then bounced to ~$100-103 range. Market cautiously optimistic — VIX dropped 13.5% on Mar 17. DOE Wed report (Mar 19) = KEY data point for physical drawdown rate. 172M bbl US + 228M global. DOE arranged 200M bbl replacement within year. Max physical pump rate 4.4 mbd. Asia-Pacific reserves available immediately per IEA.
SPR D+2.5. No delivery data. DOE Wed report Mar 19 = KEY.

SPR D+3. Still no physical delivery confirmation. Brent range $98-$103 (commodity.com $98.51, stockmarketwatch $102.82 — divergence due to Iran offensive reaction). WTI $92-$95. DOE Wed report Mar 19 = FIRST drawdown data. CNBC: disruptions could push Brent to $110-$120 in base case (4-6 weeks). Gas $3.79 avg (+27% since war start per AAA). Diesel $5/gal (CNBC Mar 17). California gas >$5/gal. Market verdict: Iran coordinated offensive will pressure oil higher; SPR ramp-up urgency increasing. 172M bbl US + 228M global, max pump 4.4 mbd.

SPR D+3. DOE Wednesday report Mar 19 = TODAY, first drawdown data expected. No physical delivery confirmed yet. Brent SURGING: .26 (oilpriceapi), .78 (Fortune at 9:15am ET Mar 18) — divergence reflects South Pars strike reaction. Pre-crisis SPR 395.3M bbl (Wikipedia Mar 7) or 415M bbl (TheWorldData). 172M bbl US drawdown announced. Gas .47/gal AAA, .70 EIA mid-March. KEY: South Pars strike will push Brent higher, making SPR urgency even greater. CNBC base case - for 4-6 weeks now looking conservative.

SPR D+3.5. DOE Wednesday report Mar 19 = TODAY. Brent $107-108 (commodity.com $107.12, ET $108/bbl). WTI $97-98 (oilpriceapi $98.10, commodity.com $98.16). Gas $3.84 national avg AAA (Fortune Mar 18, new high since 2023). Diesel ~$5/gal. CA >$5/gal. SPR 172M bbl release began Mar 16, first physical barrels expected ~Mar 21. Axios: Gallego pressing DOE on reserve adequacy. DOE arranged 200M bbl replacement within year (Politico). 🔴 CRITICAL: Ras Laffan extensive damage + Gulf energy infrastructure under fire = SPR release becoming STRUCTURALLY INSUFFICIENT. Even at max pump rate (4.4 mbd), cannot compensate for loss of Qatar LNG exports + potential Saudi/UAE production disruption. Market will reprice dramatically — CNBC $110-120 base case now CONSERVATIVE. $120-140 range if Gulf production materially disrupted.

SPR D+4 late. 🔴 DESPERATE PIVOTS: (1) ★ Bessent on Fox Business: US 'may unsanction Iranian oil' on water — ~130-140M barrels floating (Bloomberg/NYT/Newsweek/Investing.com Mar 19). This is UNPRECEDENTED: unsanctioning oil from the country you're actively bombing; (2) Bessent also said US could release MORE from SPR beyond 172M bbl already announced; (3) Bessent emphasized US 'not attacking Iranian energy infrastructure, allowing oil to continue flowing' — directly contradicts Trump's Truth Social threat to 'massively blow up entire South Pars'; (4) DOE Wednesday report Mar 19 — no specific drawdown numbers published yet in evening searches; first physical barrels expected ~Mar 21; (5) Brent SPIKED to $119 intraday (BBC/Reuters) before pulling back to ~$112 — BREACHED the $110-120 band from CNBC base case; (6) UK gas prices +19% in single day (BBC); (7) RBN Energy analysis: SPR drawdown of 172M bbl over 120 days = ~1.4 mbd avg, FAR below max 4.4 mbd pump rate; (8) Market in 'pronounced backwardation' = traders fear ACUTE near-term scarcity (FinancialContent/ChronicleJournal); (9) IER/IBTimes: Trump admin eliminated energy modeling experts before war — reduced DOE's ability to forecast market impacts; (10) India's SPR system not yet online for crisis release (APN News). CRITICAL ASSESSMENT: The Bessent unsanctioning gambit is a SIGNAL OF DESPERATION. It reveals: (a) SPR release is STRUCTURALLY INSUFFICIENT to contain prices; (b) the administration has NO good options — destroy Iran's energy = prices spike, unsanction Iran's oil = admit the war is self-defeating; (c) the internal contradiction is now PUBLIC: Trump threatens South Pars destruction while Bessent tries to flood market with Iranian oil. This is the clearest signal yet that COST THRESHOLD HAS BEEN CROSSED at the policy level.

SPR D+4. DOE Wed report Mar 19 = TODAY (no drawdown data confirmed in evening searches yet). Brent BROKE $110: $108.93 (commodity.com) to $111.36 (LiveMint, +3.71%). WTI $96.42-$98. WTI-Brent spread widest in 11 YEARS (Reuters) = international crude premium for Gulf risk surging. Gas $3.84 national avg AAA (new 2023+ high). India Sensex crashed 2,500 pts (LiveMint). European equities -2% to year low (Bloomberg). Asian stocks -2.8% (Bloomberg). S&P 500 6,624.70 (-1.36%), below 200-DMA. Dow worst month since 2022. 🔴 CRITICAL: SPR release now STRUCTURALLY OVERWHELMED. CNBC $110-120 base case has been BREACHED in <24 hours (Brent $111.36). Next band: $120-140 if Gulf production disruption persists. 172M bbl US release = drops in the bucket when Qatar LNG offline + South Pars damaged + UAE gas shut + insurance dead. Even at max pump rate (4.4 mbd), SPR addresses CRUDE supply only — LNG/gas has NO strategic reserve equivalent. Europe gets 12-14% of LNG from Qatar (Wikipedia Hormuz crisis page).

SPR D+4 late → D+5. 🔴 STRUCTURALLY OVERWHELMED: (1) Brent SPIKED $119/bbl intraday (BBC/Reuters/BNN) before pullback to ~$112 — BREACHED $110-120 band; (2) Bessent unsanctioning 130-140M bbl Iranian oil (Bloomberg/NYT Mar 19) = DESPERATION — bombing country AND unsanctioning its oil; (3) Bessent: may release MORE SPR beyond 172M bbl; (4) US oil loan from SPR depends on "stiff premiums" — DOE took bids (BOE Report Mar 18); (5) RBN Energy: 172M bbl over 120 days = ~1.4 mbd avg, FAR below max 4.4 mbd; (6) Gas $3.84 national avg AAA (Fortune/AP Mar 18) — highest since 2023; (7) WTI $95.50-$97.30 (commodity.com/247WallSt); (8) Gold $4,536-$4,605 range (Fortune/LiteFinance); (9) S&P 500 below 200-DMA for first time since May 2025 (Forbes); Dow worst month since 2022; (10) Market in pronounced backwardation = acute scarcity; (11) AJE: $150-$200/bbl now seriously discussed by analysts. CRITICAL: SPR release STRUCTURALLY INSUFFICIENT — addresses crude only, NO strategic reserve for LNG/gas. Qatar 17% LNG capacity lost 3-5 years, Kuwait refineries on fire, UAE gas offline = SPR cannot compensate for multi-fuel production destruction.

SPR D+5. MARKET CLOSE Mar 19: Brent settled $108.65 (+1.18%) after intraday spike to $119.13 — wildest intraday swing of the war (Reuters). WTI settled $96.14 (slight loss). Market PARED gains after Netanyahu agreed to halt South Pars strikes. (1) S&P 500 closed 6,606.49 (-0.27%), pared from much steeper intraday losses (CNBC); (2) Gold DOWN ~4% to $4,569-4,650 range — liquidation sell-off (Investopedia); (3) VIX surged to 25.09 — 2-year high (FinancialContent); (4) Gas $3.84/gal AAA national avg — highest since 2023; (5) No DOE Wednesday drawdown numbers confirmed in searches — first physical barrels expected ~Mar 21; (6) Bessent unsanctioning gambit still in play: 130-140M bbl Iranian oil floating (Bloomberg/NYT); (7) Pentagon $200B supplemental request (AP/WashPost confirmed); (8) RBN: 172M bbl / 120 days = ~1.4 mbd actual rate, far below 4.4 mbd max. CRITICAL: The $119→$108.65 intraday reversal was triggered by Netanyahu South Pars halt. If energy de-escalation holds, Brent may stabilize $105-115 range. But Iran's continued Gulf attacks (Yanbu/Qatar/Kuwait) mean supply destruction ongoing — SPR structurally insufficient for multi-fuel, multi-country production losses.

SPR D+5 pipeline. FRIDAY ASIAN SESSION: (1) Brent ~$105-107 in early Friday Asian trade (Upstox: $105 zone; Gulf News: $107.1, -1.45%; FXStreet WTI: ~$93.50); (2) ME crude SURGING to $131 vs Brent $107 — $24 spread = DUAL MARKET (Gulf News Mar 20); (3) S&P 500 futures gaining after Thu -0.27% close (LiveMint); (4) India Sensex rebounding +1%, Nifty +1.13% on crude decline (Business Standard/LiveMint); (5) MSCI Asia Pacific +0.3% after -2.6% (LiveMint); (6) Gas: $3.81/gal national avg (Finder Mar 19, slight pullback from $3.84); (7) Gold: $4,569-$4,650 (down ~4% from war high, liquidation); (8) VIX: ~22 (LA Times Mar 19, retreated from 35 peak on Mar 9, but was 25.09 intraday Thu); (9) DOE Wed report — still no confirmed drawdown volume published. First physical barrels expected Mar 21 (tomorrow). CRITICAL: Friday Asian session showing RELIEF RALLY on Netanyahu South Pars halt + de-escalation signals. BUT: ME crude at $131 vs Brent $107 = $24 regional premium = market pricing in PERMANENT Gulf supply risk even while international benchmarks moderate. SPR remains structurally insufficient — addresses crude only, no LNG/gas reserve. Qatar 17% LNG capacity lost 3-5yr cannot be compensated.

SPR D+5 evening. (1) First physical barrels expected TOMORROW Mar 21; (2) CBS: release over 120 days brings US reserves to 243M bbl — LOWEST IN 44 YEARS (down 41% from 415M); (3) Brent settled $108.65 Thu (after $119.13 intraday high), Friday Asian ~$105-107; (4) ★★ GOLD CRASHING: worst week since Feb 1983, down ~10% WTD (CNBC). COMEX gold $4,536-4,681 range. Liquidation sell-off, NOT safe-haven buying = inflation/liquidity fears overtaking geopolitical premium; (5) S&P 500: 6,606.49 (-0.27% Thu), below 200-DMA. Dow worst month since 2022; (6) VIX: 25.09 intraday (2-year high, FinancialContent); (7) $200B Pentagon supplemental formally requested — Politico: 'Congress braces', CNN: 'cracks emerge in GOP'; (8) NPR: 'Congress has NOT voted to approve the war'; (9) Bessent unsanctioning 130-140M bbl Iranian oil still in play; (10) ME crude $131 vs Brent $107 = $24 DUAL MARKET premium persists (Gulf News). CRITICAL: Gold crash is a REGIME SIGNAL — market no longer treating war as simple risk-off (gold up) but as INFLATIONARY SUPPLY DESTRUCTION (gold liquidated for margin/liquidity). This is what happened in March 2020 but for the OPPOSITE reason.

focal_signals.description: 焦点层信号。告诉你'什么时候转'。按优先级排序

fp1_china_action.label: 中国实质行动(唯一可行聚点注入)
fp1_china_action.why: 美伊双边无法自发找到停止点。中国是唯一有经济杠杆(伊朗最大客户)+安理会否决权+双方都不反对的第三方
observe: PLAN海军向海湾方向部署(AIS/卫星)
习近平/王毅对伊朗提出具体框架(不是'呼吁和平'的空话)
中国对伊朗石油进口实际变化(海关数据/tanker tracking)
中国与美方的秘密接触泄露
fp1_china_action.false_alarm_filter: 外交部'呼吁克制'= 噪声。需要看到成本行动(调兵/经济杠杆/特使)
fp1_china_action.findings: 🔴 SHUTTLE ACTIVE + CONDEMNATION ESCALATING BUT NO COST ACTIONS: (1) China MFA Lin Jian CONDEMNED Israel targeting Iranian leaders — 'opposes use of force', killing state leaders 'even more unacceptable' (NBC Mar 20). Rhetoric escalating from boilerplate; (2) Wang Yi met MBS in Riyadh — 'bolstering Kingdom ties with Iran' (ChinaGlobalSouth); (3) Zhai Jun shuttle: Saudi/UAE/Bahrain/Kuwait/Egypt (MFA/Firstpost); (4) Wikipedia 'China in 2026 Iran war': 'Beijing dispatched envoys for mediation and warned of spreading flames of war, while evacuating nationals'; (5) Oman FM confirmed pre-war negotiations had been near deal (Wikipedia); (6) BUT: NO PLA Navy movement, NO Xi personal intervention, NO concrete framework, NO economic leverage deployed. ASSESSMENT: China's posture is MEDIATION-ONLY with escalating rhetoric. Qatar's 17% LNG damage (Qatar = ~20% China LNG) should be forcing China's hand but hasn't yet. The gap between WHAT CHINA IS LOSING (LNG, shipping, economic stability) and WHAT CHINA IS DOING (sending envoys) is the widest it's been. If China doesn't act with cost actions in next 72 hours, fp1 may need downgrade from 5-star to 4-star priority — China choosing to absorb losses rather than intervene.

fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.label: 美方信号包内部矛盾(承诺解锁前导)
fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.why: 前景理论: Trump承诺弹性>IRGC。解锁更可能从美方开始
observe: 修辞仍强硬但军事行动转向护航编队(言行分裂)
Bessent做市场稳定化 vs Pentagon升级(政府内部分裂)
任命谈判代表(与'不谈判'矛盾)
军事优先级从打击转向护航(资源分配信号)
SPR释放令(高成本行动=不只是说说)
fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.current_assessment: ★★★★★ 3/18 07:22 — EXIT_SIGNALING confirmed multi-source: (1) Trump 3/17 Reuters: 'we'll be leaving in pretty much the very near future' — first explicit exit timeline signal; (2) Trump claims 'wiped out Iran's military, navy and air force' = victory narrative for exit; (3) Blames NATO allies for Hormuz failure = scapegoat construction; (4) Claims 'we don't need any help' — contradicts Energy Sec 'we're simply not ready'; (5) NBC 3/17: Pentagon presenting daily options to end war, Trump hasn't taken any yet — suggesting imminent decision; (6) Araghchi (Fars/NBC): 'We did not request ceasefire but war must end in a way it will not be repeated' — Iran opening door without appearing to beg; (7) Kent resignation still reverberating — MAGA anti-war faction legitimized; (8) $11.3B first 6 days (Pentagon to Congress) = unsustainable burn rate; (9) VIX dropped 13.5% Mar 17 + Brent briefly below $100 = market cautiously pricing exit; (10) Trump postpones China visit (Indian Express). Pattern: five EXIT vectors converging — economic/military/diplomatic/political/narrative. Key: this is NOT a negotiation setup. It's a unilateral 'declare victory and leave' architecture. No ceasefire agreement needed — just a presidential announcement.
fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.findings: 🔴🔴🔴 WAR STRATEGY BIFURCATION — DESTROY + REOPEN SIMULTANEOUSLY: (1) ★★★ ACTION: A-10 Warthogs deployed to Hormuz hunting fast attack craft (Gen. Caine Mar 19) — FIRST dedicated maritime reopening operations; (2) ★★★ ACTION: Trump halted Netanyahu on South Pars (CNN/AP Mar 19) — FIRST constraint on Israel targeting; (3) ★★ Reuters: US weighing 'thousands of troops' including Iran SHORELINE deployment — ESCALATION while simultaneously preparing REOPENING; (4) ★★ $200B Pentagon supplemental formally requested (AP/WashPost/CNBC/Politico Mar 19) — Politico: 'Congress braces', CNN: 'cracks emerge in GOP'; (5) NPR: 'Congress has NOT voted to approve the war'; (6) CIA Ratcliffe: 4-6 weeks total (NYT/BBC) — 1-3 more from now; (7) Netanyahu: Iran 'can no longer enrich uranium or make BMs' = EXIT NARRATIVE LOCKED (AP Mar 19); (8) Kent resignation: 'no imminent threat' = legal basis eroding (Guardian); (9) DailyMail Mar 20: 'US opens NEW FRONT' at Hormuz — jets obliterate Iranian ships; (10) Spectator: European navies 'too small, too slow' for Hormuz escorts. SYNTHESIS: US strategy has SPLIT into two parallel tracks: (A) DEGRADATION: continuing 7,000+ target strikes, Caspian expansion, F-35 ops; (B) REOPENING: A-10s at Hormuz, 31st MEU, possible troop deployment. These two tracks create INTERNAL CONTRADICTION: you can't 'reopen' a strait while bombing the country that controls its coastline. EXIT PROBABILITY: 55-60% within 14 days via unilateral 'mission accomplished'. ESCALATION: 25% (ground troops on Iran shoreline). STALEMATE: 15-20%.

fp3_face_saving_framework.label: 面子工程原材料出现
fp3_face_saving_framework.why: 需要让双方都能宣称'赢了'的框架才能退出
observe: UN安理会决议草案(含具体框架,不是空谈)
'霍尔木兹自由通行协议'类提案
任何'条件换条件'的交易结构出现
阿曼/卡塔尔宣布促成某种安排
fp3_face_saving_framework.current_assessment: 退出框架变异: (1) 传统双边停火 = still not_detected; (2) BUT Trump's unilateral 'declare victory and leave' architecture = EMERGING. Reuters 3/14: US rebuffed Mideast allies' ceasefire attempts. NBC: Pentagon daily options presented but none taken. Trump Mar 15 (Guardian): 'not ready to make a deal' but Iran 'keen to negotiate'. Trump Mar 17 (Reuters): 'leaving in very near future'. Araghchi (Fars): 'we did not request ceasefire but war must end'. (3) Larijani killed = moderate channel closed. (4) Mojtaba inner circle = all IRGC hardliners (NYT). (5) UN Security Council: condemned Iran but rejected Russia ceasefire proposal. fp3 being SHORT-CIRCUITED by fp2: Trump doesn't need bilateral framework — only a 'victory moment' to announce withdrawal. Risk: withdrawal without ceasefire = unstable. Mines remain, insurance frozen, proxy attacks continue. 'Ending the war' ≠ 'resolving the crisis'.
fp3_face_saving_framework.findings: STATUS: DEAD BILATERAL — UNILATERAL EXIT CRYSTALLIZING: (1) Araghchi firmly rejects ceasefire (Time Mar 18); (2) Netanyahu: 'mission accomplished' nuclear narrative LOCKED (AP); (3) ISW Mar 18: 'Neither US nor Iranian officials currently appear to be seeking a ceasefire agreement'; (4) Wikipedia negotiations page: Oman FM says pre-war deal was 'on the verge'; (5) China shuttle active but no concrete framework; (6) $200B request = Congress now a potential EXIT MECHANISM via funding denial (4 War Powers resolutions eligible in 10 days); (7) House of Commons Library (UK): comprehensive briefing on war, noting UK base use + strikes; (8) Spectator: 'Let their navies figure it out' — US pushing allies who can't deliver. ASSESSMENT: Formal bilateral ceasefire probability <5%. UNILATERAL US EXIT probability 55-60% within 14 days. NEW PATH: Congress-forced exit via War Powers or funding denial — $200B request creates POLITICAL leverage point. 4 remaining resolutions + bipartisan cost concerns (CNN/Politico) = growing constraint.

fp4_cost_threshold.label: 成本阈值的物理逼近
fp4_cost_threshold.why: 成本本身不是信号，成本逼近政治不可承受点才是

us_side: 国会War Powers投票排期(有没有排期?)
汽油$5持续2周后的民调数据
Pentagon累计成本报告($19B/天→国会反弹)
美军中东基地被迫撤离/加固
iran_side: IRGC攻击类型从导弹退化到只有无人机/快艇(能力阈值)
德黑兰断水/断电/燃料短缺报道
伊朗内部抗议规模升级
IRGC某区域完全停止行动(碎片化到失控)

fp4_cost_threshold.current_assessment: 3/18 07:22 — 成本非线性加速: 【美方】(1) $11.3B first 6 days (Pentagon→Congress) at ~$1.9B/day → Day 19 extrapolated ~$36B total; (2) Gas +80¢/month (NPR), diesel $5/gal; (3) Axios: gas price shock hitting 2026 Senate battleground states hardest; (4) Reuters: 'rising gas prices imperil Trump's Republican majority'; (5) Trump public exit timeline ('very near future') = COST THRESHOLD CROSSED politically; (6) VIX -13.5% = market pricing in exit probability; (7) Brent briefly <$100 = partial relief signal; (8) Amnesty school strike report (100+ children) = intl reputation cost; (9) Kent resignation = MAGA antiwar legitimized; (10) All allies refused Hormuz escort = no cost-sharing. 【伊方】(1) Larijani+Basij cmdr killed — continued decapitation; (2) Mojtaba inner circle = all IRGC hardliners → regime extremization not collapse; (3) IRGC vessel attacks ceased 6 days but Sejjil BM + Iraq proxy = capability adapting; (4) Internet crackdown + Starlink suppression = internal control; (5) IranWire: IRGC views war as 'unifying tool'. 关键: US political cost threshold CROSSED (Trump publicly signaling exit). Iran using war for internal cohesion. Asymmetric cost structure → US exits first (high confidence 75%+).
fp4_cost_threshold.findings: 🔴🔴 COST REGIME CHANGE — GOLD CRASH SIGNALS PARADIGM SHIFT: 【MARKET REGIME】(1) ★★★ Gold: WORST WEEK SINCE FEB 1983, down ~10% WTD (CNBC). $4,536-$4,681/oz range. Liquidation, NOT safe-haven = market treating war as INFLATIONARY DESTRUCTION not risk-off event; (2) Brent: $108.65 settle Thu after $119.13 intraday (wildest swing of war). Friday Asian ~$105-107; (3) ME crude: $131 — $24 premium vs Brent = PERMANENT DUAL MARKET (Gulf News); (4) WTI: ~$93.50-$96 (FXStreet); (5) S&P 500: 6,606.49 (-0.27% Thu), below 200-DMA. Futures gaining Friday; (6) VIX: 25.09 (2-year high). Dow worst month since 2022; (7) MSCI Asia Pacific: +0.3% Friday (relief rally). noise_filter.rule: 按行为类型分类,不按人物分类
noise: 巴盖伊等发言人声明(言辞)
媒体'消息人士'报道(未验证)
signal: Trump签署行政令/军事命令(行动)
美军实际调兵/部署变化(行动)
IRGC实际攻击模式变化(行动)
中国实际经济/军事动作(行动)
noise_filter.key_distinction: 同一个人的言辞=噪声,行动=信号。Trump说'just for fun'=噪声。Trump命令打Kharg=信号。

pipeline_daily_checklist: 1. 物理层: 更新Q1(袭击频率) + Q2(通行量) + Q3(SPR释放)
2. 焦点层: 扫描fp1(中国动作) + fp2(美方矛盾) + fp3(退出框架) + fp4(成本阈值)
3. 噪声过滤: 新闻分类为action vs rhetoric
4. 结构性验证: 跑structural_verify检查盲区+停滞+链断裂
5. DAG更新: 只有物理层变化或焦点层信号触发才更新概率
